_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — within-tier delta from L7 'Basic Plate' keeper to L8 'Mid Plate + First Tassets + Gold Helm Trim + Half-Visor'. Two-image input: image_urls[0] = L7 v3 keeper (content reference), image_urls[1] = L1_Base.png (scale/framing/pose anchor only). Dagger retires here with the wider belt + tassets.
_meta.input_image_content: UnitProgression/Archer/out/v3/L7/composite/archer_L7_v<keeper>_composited.png (v3 chain, user picks keeper at runtime)
_meta.level_beat_doc: UnitProgression/Archer/Archer_Progression_Plan.md (L8 — Mid Plate; revised 2026-05-07 per AD FeedbackV0)
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chain (single-input chained, locked 2026-05-07) — previous keeper composite is the sole input. No L1_Base scale anchor.
history: v1-v_visor_lames (2026-05-04 / 05): see prior history — gold helm trim, tassets, half-visor, layered pauldrons + rivets, wider belt.
v_no_stubble (2026-05-05): Stripped 'faint stubble shadow'. Canonical face is clean-shaven.

v2_detail_economy (2026-05-07): MAJOR REWRITE per AD FeedbackV0 (CLAUDE.md Part 3 §23 + Part 2 §I). (a) DROPPED brass rivets at the pauldron seam (per detail-economy 'cut filigree/rivets first' rule). Layered two-plate pauldron stays as the volume bump but with NO rivets — clean steel-on-steel seam only. (b) STRENGTHENED gold trim band on helm with full polished-gold rendering language per CLAUDE.md Part 2 §I (saturated buttery-yellow base + soft top-down highlight reading almost white-yellow + deeper warm shadow on underside reading honey-orange — IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale; addresses Infantry-L8 first-gold soft-fail pattern). (c) DAGGER SHEATH RETIRES at L8 (per AD locked decision — surfaced in keeper review per CLAUDE.md Part 3 §17). (d) PRESERVE block updated to reflect v3 L7 state: FULL-arm scale mail (no bicep gap — only thin scale-mail visible between pauldron lower edge and vambrace upper edge at bicep/elbow region); RED MANTLE at shoulders (not red cloth coif/scarf); scale-mail coif at neck retired at L7; bow at FULL LONGBOW proportions wood-brown (no gold inner-curve inlay — that beat was dropped at L6). (e) Updated paths to v3 chain. Adds count 5 (gold helm trim / tassets / half-visor / layered pauldron volume bump / wider belt + dagger retires).

v3_single_input (2026-05-07): Dropped L1_Base from image_urls per locked single-input chained rule (CLAUDE.md Part 1 §B revised). Stripped 'SECOND input image' / scale-anchor language from prompt body and FRAMING block heading. Sole input is the previous composited keeper. urls_before=2, urls_after=1, body_chars_removed=47.

v4_dagger_restored (2026-05-08): Per AD feedback — REVERSED the L8 dagger retirement. The dagger sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip carries through L8, L9, L9.5, L10 (was retired at L8 in v2_detail_economy). Add (5) wider waist belt no longer 'occupies the dagger space' — the wider belt simply accommodates the dagger sheath above the tassets. PRESERVE block adds dagger sheath back; NEGATIVE block reverses 'DO NOT keep the dagger sheath' → 'DO NOT remove the dagger sheath'. CLAUDE.md Part 2 §H + Part 3 §23 also updated to reflect the carry-through.

prompt: EDIT GOAL — Take the archer in the input image (open-face conical steel helm, steel breastplate covering collarbones to mid-torso, steel vambraces on both forearms with thin gold trim line at the wrist edge, single-piece steel pauldron domes at both shoulders, FULL-ARM scale mail covering bicep through elbow through forearm with the vambrace sitting on top at the forearm — no bare bicep, mustard tunic with red panels visible as a horizontal livery band between the breastplate and the wider leather belt, dagger sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip standalone, leather greaves below the knee, brown leather boots with steel toe cap (carried from L6), brown back-quiver with white-feather red-tip arrows, FULL LONGBOW proportions wood-brown bow with brass grip-end bindings + brass tip caps, red CLOTH MANTLE at the shoulders / back of neck, crimson cape, olive trousers) and apply FIVE clearly-visible upgrades to produce the 'Mid Plate + First Tassets + Gold Helm Trim + Half-Visor' variant. The character is the same archer in the same pose at the same scale and framing — only the five adds change. ===== NEW ADDS FOR THIS EDIT ===== (1) THIN HORIZONTAL GOLD TRIM BAND ON THE HELM (FIRST GOLD ON THE HEAD) — a thin (about half-inch tall) horizontal band of polished BUTTERY YELLOW GOLD METAL wraps around the entire conical steel helm at roughly TWO-THIRDS of the way up from the helm's brow rim toward the apex. The band cleanly separates the lower body of the helm from the upper cone. Plain band — no engraving, no rivets, no gemstones, no decorative motifs — just a smooth gold strip. GOLD RENDERING: this band must read as POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL — saturated warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against the cool silver-gray steel of the helm body, distinctly more yellow than steel, NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard tint, NOT a warm-tinted highlight on steel. Render with: (i) saturated buttery-yellow base across the band's full width; (ii) soft top-down highlight that reads almost white-yellow at the upper edge; (iii) deeper warm shadow on the underside that reads honey-orange. The gold band must be IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale. The helm's silver-steel body otherwise unchanged (open-face below the half-visor, conical, low rounded apex, no finial yet). (2) ALTERNATING RED + MUSTARD-YELLOW TASSETS AT THE HIP — a row of vertical CLOTH STRIPS hangs from the wider waistline DOWN TO MID-THIGH, ALTERNATING crimson-red and mustard-yellow strips around the hip silhouette. Each strip is roughly 1.5 to 2 inches wide and about 6 to 7 inches long, with subtle vertical fold lines (stiffened cloth, not flowing). The strips alternate red-yellow-red-yellow-red across the front of the hip and continue around the sides. The tassets are the FIRST appearance of the red+yellow alternating motif and migrate the heraldic livery from the chest (where it has been from the start) to the hip silhouette. Both colors must be CLEARLY VISIBLE and read as alternating strips at thumbnail scale. (3) STEEL HALF-VISOR WITH HORIZONTAL EYE SLITS COVERS THE UPPER FACE — the open-face conical steel helm from the input image now has a fixed steel half-visor attached at the front rim of the helm. The half-visor is a single curved plate of the same polished cool silver-gray steel as the helm, hinged at the temple line and extending DOWN from the helm rim across the brow and the upper face, ending at the upper cheekbone level (mid-cheek line, well above the upper lip). Two horizontal narrow eye slits are cut through the visor at the eye line — each slit about 0.75 inches tall and 1.25 inches wide, positioned over each eye, with a faint warm dark brown glow visible inside the slit suggesting the eye behind it. The visor's lower edge is a clean horizontal line across the cheekbones with a slight downward curve over the bridge of the nose. CRITICAL — THE LOWER FACE REMAINS FULLY VISIBLE: the cheeks below the cheekbone, the small upturned nose tip, the closed-mouth half-smirk, the jawline and the chin all remain CLEARLY VISIBLE below the lower edge of the half-visor. The peachy-tan skin tone, the rosy blush on the cheeks, and the rosy nose tip all remain exactly as in the input image. The eyebrows and the eyes themselves are now COVERED by the half-visor (only visible as the warm dark glow inside the eye slits). The half-visor's surface is plain polished steel with a soft top-down highlight; no engraving, no gold trim on the visor itself (the gold trim is at the helm's main band per add (1)), no decorative embossing. The visor reads as a fixed protective face plate that bridges the open-face helm of the previous level and the fully-closed visor at the next level. (4) LAYERED TWO-PLATE PAULDRONS (VOLUME BUMP, NO RIVETS) — the single-piece dome steel pauldron caps from the input image grow a SECOND lower lame attached below the upper cap on BOTH shoulders. Each pauldron is now TWO STACKED PLATES: an upper dome cap (the original cap, unchanged in shape) AND a new smaller secondary lame curving below it that covers the upper bicep area, extending the pauldron coverage about 2-3 inches further down the bicep. There is a CLEAN VISIBLE SEAM/GAP between the upper cap and the lower lame, painted as a thin shadow line where the plates meet. Both plates are the same polished cool silver-gray steel as the original cap, with bright leading-edge highlights. The seam is a CLEAN STEEL-ON-STEEL line — NO rivets, NO brass dots, NO gold trim, NO decorative beading along the seam. The lower lame's edge is a clean curved line where it meets the upper bicep / scale-mail sleeve. This is a VOLUME BUMP at the shoulder silhouette — the pauldrons read as taller and more substantial without surface ornament. (5) WIDER LEATHER WAIST BELT WITH BRASS RECTANGULAR BUCKLE — the wide leather belt is upgraded to a SUBSTANTIALLY WIDER warm-brown leather waist belt (about 1.5x the prior belt's height — roughly 3-4 inches tall now), sitting just below the breastplate's lower edge and ANCHORING the new tassets. Brass rectangular buckle centered at the front, slightly larger than the prior buckle. Visible edge stitching along both upper and lower edges of the belt. The DAGGER SHEATH and dark-wood dagger handle from the input image CARRY THROUGH — the dagger sheath hangs from the wider belt at the anatomical-RIGHT hip, sitting above the tassets at that position (the tassets part naturally around the sheath OR the sheath sits in front of the tassets at that hip — either reading is acceptable). The belt is otherwise clean apart from the dagger — no pouches, no satchels, no tool loops. The livery band (mustard tunic with red panels) between the breastplate and the belt is COMPRESSED but still VISIBLE — a thinner band (about 2-3 inches tall instead of 4-6) of mustard-yellow with the red center panel still readable above the wider belt. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== the open-face conical steel helm shape (now with the new gold trim band per add (1) and the new half-visor per add (3), but no other change to the helm — same silver-steel body, same low rounded apex, no finial); the red CLOTH MANTLE at the shoulders / back of the neck (the crimson-red mantle that drapes at the shoulders from the input image, carried unchanged — visible behind the helm rim at the back of the neck); the lower face fully visible — chibi jawline + clean-shaven smooth peachy-tan skin + closed-mouth half-smirk with viewer-left corner pulled higher + small slightly upturned nose with rosy tint on the tip + warm peachy-tan skin tone with rosy blush on the cheeks (the upper face — eyes + brow — is COVERED by the new half-visor per add (3)); the polished steel breastplate covering the upper chest from collarbones to mid-torso, plain polished plate with soft top-down gradient and clean central highlight; the mustard-yellow tunic with the red center panel and red side panels visible as a compressed horizontal livery band between the breastplate's lower edge and the top of the new wider belt (now narrower than at the previous level — but mustard color and red center panel still readable); both steel vambraces from wrist to just below the elbow, polished silver-gray steel with longitudinal seam visible on the outer side, with the thin BUTTERY GOLD trim line at each wrist edge; the FULL-ARM scale mail covering each arm from deltoid down to wrist (chunky overlapping cool silver-gray scale plates per the established scale-mail visual spec) — visible at the bicep + elbow between the new layered pauldron's lower lame above and the steel vambrace's upper edge below (NO bare-skin gap on either arm); both hands BARE peachy-tan skin (no gloves, no gauntlets); the FULL LONGBOW held vertically in his anatomical-LEFT hand at hip height, with warm wood-brown limbs (full longbow length from the previous level), dark-brown leather grip wrap, brass binding rings at each end of the leather grip, brass tip caps wrapping the outermost half-inch of each limb tip — NO gold anywhere on the bow; the brown leather cylindrical back-quiver slung diagonally across the back over his anatomical-LEFT shoulder rising behind the viewer-RIGHT side of the head, three to four arrows visible with WHITE feather fletching and RED bindings and RED nock tips and pale wood-tan shafts; the crimson-red knee-length cape attached at both shoulders falling with soft painted vertical folds; the olive / muted forest-green knee-length cloth trousers (partially covered by the leather greaves below the knee, but the trouser color still visible above the knee where the new tassets stop at mid-thigh); the dark-wood dagger in its leather sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip with the brass capstone on the handle (carried through from the input image — the sheath hangs from the new wider belt above/in-front-of the tassets); the warm-brown leather wrap-style greaves on the lower legs from boot cuff to just below the knee with single cross-strap binding; the chunky warm-brown leather boots with the darker folded ankle cuff — including the SMALL STEEL TOE CAP wrapping the front toe of each boot (carried from L6, unchanged at this level). ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately 15-25 degrees to the viewer's left so the character's anatomical right side faces camera, weight planted evenly on both feet with a very slight forward lean. THE BOW ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) hangs DOWN at his side with the elbow only slightly bent, the bow held VERTICALLY at HIP height directly in front of the hip with the hand gripping the bow's central leather grip wrap. THE DRAW ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) is bent sharply at the elbow at roughly 90 degrees, with the FOREARM HELD HORIZONTALLY ACROSS THE CHEST at upper-chest / sternum level (parallel to the ground). The vambraced hand sits at the CENTER OF THE CHEST area in front of the steel breastplate, slightly toward the bow side, in a TIGHT CLOSED FIST shape with the KNUCKLES facing OUTWARD toward the viewer. The fist does NOT touch the bow, the bowstring, or the breastplate. Calm balanced HERO STANCE; NOT an active draw, NOT a nocking motion, NOT a reach. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 75% of canvas vertical height (~765px tall) and approximately 57% of canvas horizontal width (~580px wide). TOP of helm at ~13.5% from top of canvas (~138px headroom). BOTTOM of boots at ~12% from bottom of canvas (~120px below). Character roughly vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the helm or feet, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire character in deep warm brown-black with even weight, slight thickening in shadowed recesses, slight thinning under the key light. Interior linework soft and value-driven, never hard black. Clean white background with no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT cover the lower face — the cheeks, the small upturned nose tip, the closed-mouth half-smirk, the jawline, and the chin must all remain CLEARLY VISIBLE below the half-visor's lower edge; DO NOT extend the half-visor down past the cheekbones; DO NOT cover the mouth or jaw with steel; DO NOT make the eye slits round or vertical — the slits are HORIZONTAL narrow slots, one per eye; DO NOT make the half-visor a full closed-face mask (the closed visor arrives at the next level); DO NOT add gold trim, gold band, or gold accents to the half-visor surface; DO NOT engrave the half-visor or add decorative motifs; DO NOT change the helm shape — only the new gold trim band and the new half-visor are added; DO NOT add a finial, spike, plume, feather, crest, or any apex ornament on the helm at this level (the gold finial arrives at the next levels); DO NOT engrave, etch, emboss, rivet, or decorate the gold helm trim band — it is a plain smooth gold strip; DO NOT render the gold helm trim as brass / bronze / yellow paint / mustard tint / a warm highlight on steel — it must read as POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL distinctly more yellow than steel; DO NOT add any second gold accent beyond the helm trim band and the existing thin gold trim at the vambrace wrist edges; DO NOT add brass rivets, brass studs, gold dots, or any seam decoration along the pauldron seam — the layered two-plate pauldron seam is a CLEAN STEEL-ON-STEEL shadow line; DO NOT add a third plate / lame to the pauldrons — exactly TWO stacked plates (upper cap + one lower lame); DO NOT add gold trim, gold band, gold rim, or any gold on the pauldrons, breastplate, vambraces (other than the existing wrist-edge trim), or belt; DO NOT replace the brass belt buckle with gold; DO NOT remove the dagger sheath or the dark-wood dagger handle — the dagger sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip CARRIES THROUGH and hangs from the new wider belt; DO NOT add hip pouches, leather satchels, tool-loop rings, or any belt accessory other than the existing dagger sheath — the wider belt carries only the brass buckle and the dagger sheath; DO NOT add gilt limb tips on the bow or any gold on the bow at this level — the bow stays brass + wood-brown; DO NOT make the tassets a continuous solid skirt or a single piece of cloth — they MUST be DISTINCT VERTICAL STRIPS with visible alternating red and mustard-yellow colors and visible vertical gaps between strips; DO NOT use only one of the livery colors on the tassets — both red and mustard-yellow MUST alternate clearly; DO NOT extend the tassets below mid-thigh into the greaves area; DO NOT cover the leather greaves below the knee with anything new; DO NOT add metal sabatons, plate boots, or any new steel covering on the feet at this level — boots remain warm-brown leather with only the carried-over L6 STEEL TOE CAP at the front toe; DO NOT add boot eyelets, lacing strings, brass cuff trim bands, or any surface trim — boot beats are PLATE accumulation only; DO NOT add gauntlets, plate gloves, or finger armor — both hands remain BARE peachy-tan skin; DO NOT cover the livery tunic band entirely with the wider belt — a thinner mustard+red band must remain visible above the belt; DO NOT replace the mustard tunic band with a neutral / brown / gray fabric; DO NOT use FINE CHAINMAIL RING MESH, interlocking metal rings, fish-scale fine texture, or scale that reads as small dots — the visible scale mail at the bicep + elbow region between pauldron and vambrace is CHUNKY OVERLAPPING ROUNDED SCALES per the established visual spec; DO NOT shrink the scale mail back to short-sleeve at the deltoid + upper bicep — at this level the scale mail is FULL-arm coverage carried over from the previous level; DO NOT leave a bare-skin band at the bicep; DO NOT swap the hand holding the bow; DO NOT shorten the bow back from full longbow proportions; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change any facial feature (lower face), hair color, skin tone, or expression; DO NOT change the cape color, length, or drape; DO NOT change the bow position; DO NOT change the arrow fletching colors (white feather + red tips); DO NOT add or remove arrows; DO NOT change the quiver position; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background or add scenery; DO NOT drift the art style; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
